quote_of_day.text: I still don't understand why Animal was hated so much. I enjoyed it more than Dhurandhar, Animal had more literally me moments with that swastika scene. Animal feels more timeless, and a very very very good story on toxic father etc situations. It was a fantastic movie
quote_of_day.name: Anon

guest_letters.title: Based website
guest_letters.body: Why aren’t we talking more about this IAC guy?? I just woke uop and checked bahut interesting site hai, there’s quotes and funny articles and board status?? We need more anons like iac to make cool shit. Im a very impressed pajeet. Writing this to see if it will get featured on the newspaper or not, im not sure how he set it up but if the !thing works then hello indus news! Im on da news niggaaaaaa
guest_letters.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather for Indus today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of sacrilegious remarks moved over the board, bringing scattered thunderstorms of blasphemy and dietary debates. Anon's comments on sacred animals and beef consumption sparked gusts of outrage.

A cold front of racist comparisons swept in, with Anon likening Sub-Saharan Africans to 'homo erectus', bringing a chill of discomfort to the discussion.

Meanwhile, a heat wave of frustration with India's leadership and development issues persisted, with criticism of the country's priorities and politics.

A series of scattered showers of inquiries and calls for British colonization also touched down, with Anon asking about board ownership and others calling for colonial rule.

articles.title: Anon debates sacred animals, dietary prohibitions
articles.body:
Anon questioned the concept of sacred animals, specifically mentioning cows, saying it allows other groups to mock those who revere them.

The original poster, Savarkar-pilled, argued that having a sacred animal is 'retarded' and cited eating beef as reasonable, comparing it to other mammals that give milk. Anon responded that dietary prohibitions, such as not eating pork, stem from disgust rather than worship.

Another anon noted that circumcision and not consuming blood from slaughtered animals are also practices that lack logic. Anon discussed the origins of circumcision, suggesting it may have been created in desert areas to conserve water for bathing.

Anon also mentioned that Muslims believe pigs are evil humans turned into pigs by Allah. The thread touched on the idea that reverence for cows allows non-Hindus to claim superiority over Hindus.

One anon proposed creating a new sect of Hinduism that consumes meat without religious restrictions.

Anon debated the merits of vegetarianism, with one arguing that it's a matter of opinion and another stating they see no point in debating whether a prohibition stems from sacredness or disgust.

The conversation also involved comparisons between dietary restrictions in different religions, with anon noting that Muslims get people arrested for posting about pork and another stating that most Hindus don't care about non-Hindus eating beef.

Anon criticized the practice of circumcision as 'retarded' and 'permanent genital mutilation.' Anon concluded that the thread wasn't about vegetarianism but rather about non-vegetarians arguing about the sanctity of cows. The discussion remained focused on the original topic, with no clear resolution.

articles.title: Anon compares Sub-Saharan Africans to 'homo erectus'
articles.body:
Anon expressed fear about people from Sub-Saharan Africa, stating that they have "0 impulse control like a homo erectus" and joked about them flooding other countries.

Another anon mentioned a documentary about a Russian alliance with the Central African Republic and noted that "nigger leaders are often incompetent af" and that they are corrupt, taking "dumb decisions".

A respondent fantasized about a sexual scenario involving Candace Owens, a political commentator, dressed in a blue saree, and described a scenario where she would chant "Jai Bhim slogans".

Anon also discussed the topic of racial purity, stating that even a little contamination with "negro blood" can bring down the mean IQ of a race, citing Brazil as an example.

They suggested that the world should "abandon them and not mix with them", calling them "not human".

Some anons shared their views on the Israel-Palestine conflict, with one stating that Israel has "raped nations for less" and another mentioning the Entebbe raid, where Israel rescued Jews from Uganda.

Anon also discussed the role of Jews in supporting and promoting people from Sub-Saharan Africa.

The conversation also touched on the topic of intelligence and societal development, with one anon stating that people from Sub-Saharan Africa are "not even human beings" and that "one third of their geno is literally non sapien".

Another respondent argued that India is "objectively far better" than Sub-Saharan Africa, citing its economic and technological advancements.

The thread also included discussions about the potential consequences of mixing with people from Sub-Saharan Africa, with one anon suggesting that it could lead to a decline in racial purity and intelligence.

articles.title: Criticism of India's Leadership and Development
articles.body:
Anon criticized India's leaders, saying they spend too much energy on scams and votebank politics instead of building factories, manufacturing, and R&D. This, according to Anon, is why India remains a laughingstock, still importing everything.

Another anon responded, suggesting that if India's leaders disappeared overnight, not much would change. This anon argued that halting reservation systems could redirect significant funds to address pressing issues like cleaning the Yamuna river and improving sewer designs.

Anon also discussed the Indian military, stating that modernizing and expanding it to a point of sustained offensive operations would require significant investment, estimated at around $100 billion over a decade.

This anon believed that with proper planning and funding, India could have a capable military. One respondent expressed frustration with the current state of the nation, citing poor sewage management, urban heat traps, and a lack of care for population growth.

This anon felt that India is doomed if it continues on its current path. Another anon suggested that the Indian government might be infiltrated and backed by foreign powers to keep the country unstable and prevent actual development.

This claim was made without evidence, but it resonated with some respondents. The discussion also touched on the military's capabilities, with one anon stating that India's military manufacturing capability is not inferior, but rather underfunded.

This anon argued that with private partnerships, India could significantly increase its military production. The thread also included harsh criticism of certain castes, with one anon making derogatory comments about Dalits and OBCs.

This respondent claimed that these groups are inherently problematic and that their elevation is a result of unfair advantages.

Overall, the discussion revolved around criticism of India's leadership, development, and societal issues, with many respondents expressing frustration and disappointment with the current state of the nation.

articles.title: Anon Inquires About 'Bharatchan', 'Gayush Dalit', and Board Ownership
articles.body:
Anon asked what happened with 'bharatchan', why someone called another user 'gayush dalit', and who owns the board. They mentioned they were offline for weeks and sought information without inciting hate.

Another anon replied that 'bharatchan' was likely referring to a past admin who had issues, including doxxing, and eventually stopped running the site due to various problems such as reports, raids, and internal conflicts.

A respondent claimed that the admin had stopped because of 'caste war' and constant issues with certain groups. They also mentioned that the admin had been doxxed after mentioning a workplace conversation and a common last name.

Anon also discussed 'motivanon', an individual who apparently got doxxed, with one user expressing regret over the incident and another suggesting it was a 'muslim trying to shill civil war in india'.

articles.title: Anon Calls for British Colonization of India
articles.body:
Anon posted a tirade against Indian society, calling Indians 'shit lovers' and 'low IQ insects.' The poster expressed a desire for the British to return and establish a 'pure European and Western meritocracy.' Anon responded with criticisms of the original post, labeling it a 'retarded movement' with 'retarded ideals.' Another poster discussed cybersecurity and potential deanonymization risks.

Other comments included proposals for counter-movements and personal attacks on perceived opponents. One anon suggested learning 'cybersecurity, osint, opsec' to counter the perceived threat.
